title: abutilon incanum subsp. pringlei
intro: Abutilon incanum subsp. pringlei, also known as Pringle's Indian mallow, is a plant species that belongs to the Malvaceae family and is native to Mexico and parts of Central America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Abutilon incanum subsp. pringlei include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to support skin and wound healing. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is prepared by making infusions, decoctions, or tinctures from its leaves and flowers, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
